content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

1. Prometheus Operator is running in the `monitoring` namespace
2. A `Prometheus` CR named `crdb` exists, configured to watch:
   - `serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: matchNames: [cockroachdb]`
   - `podMonitorNamespaceSelector: matchNames: [cockroachdb]`
   - `serviceMonitorSelector: {}` (matches all ServiceMonitors)
   - `podMonitorSelector: {}` (matches all PodMonitors)

3. CockroachDB is running in the `cockroachdb` namespace with:
   - 3 pods (`crdb-cluster-0`, `crdb-cluster-1`, `crdb-cluster-2`)
   - Headless service `crdb-cluster` and public service `crdb-cluster-public`, both exposing gRPC on port 26257 and HTTP on port 8080
   - Metrics available at `/_status/vars` on port 8080

Since the Prometheus CR already matches all ServiceMonitors in the `cockroachdb` namespace, I need to create a ServiceMonitor there that targets the CockroachDB metrics endpoint.

Now I'm checking whether the Prometheus service account has the necessary RBAC permissions to scrape metrics from the CockroachDB namespace.

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. The Prometheus CR is already configured with `serviceMonitorSelector: {}` and `serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: {matchNames: [cockroachdb]}` — I just need to create a `ServiceMonitor` in the `cockroachdb` namespace targeting the `http` port with the `/_status/vars` path.

Let me also check RBAC to ensure Prometheus can scrape across namespaces.

input.command: kubectl get clusterrolebinding | grep prometheus && kubectl get serviceaccount prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml 2>&1 | head -20
kubectl exec -n cockroachdb curl-test -- curl -s http://crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:8080/_status/vars 2>&1 | head -10
input.description: Verify CockroachDB metrics endpoint is reachable

content.thinking:
The metrics endpoint is confirmed reachable. The Prometheus ClusterRole binding also exists. Now I'll create a `ServiceMonitor` in the `cockroachdb` namespace. The Prometheus CR watches `cockroachdb` namespace for `ServiceMonitor`s with any labels (`serviceMonitorSelector: {}`).

I'll create a ServiceMonitor that:
- Selects the `crdb-cluster` service (using its labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster`)
- Scrapes port `http` (8080)
- Targets the `/_status/vars` endpoint for metrics collection

content.text: The metrics endpoint is confirmed working. Now I'll create the `ServiceMonitor` in the `cockroachdb` namespace.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: cockroachdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  endpoints:
  - port: http
    path: /_status/vars
    interval: 30s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s
EOF
input.description: Create ServiceMonitor for CockroachDB metrics scraping

content.text: Now wait for Prometheus to pick it up and verify the target becomes active.

input.command: sleep 15 && kubectl get servicemonitor cockroachdb -n cockroachdb -o yaml 2>&1 | tail -20
